population: Adult patients (>18 years) presenting to the emergency department (ED) of a tertiary-care university hospital with a diagnosis of sepsis or infection-related conditions treated with a sepsis protocol.
predictor: National Early Warning Score (NEWS), Search Out Severity (SOS) score, quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (qSOFA), Emergency Severity Index (ESI) triage, and Systemic Inflammatory Response System (SIRS) criteria.
outcome: 28-day mortality (primary); 28-day mechanical ventilator usage and 28-day vasopressor usage (secondary).
timing: Predictors measured at ED triage; outcomes assessed at 28 days.
method: Retrospective cross-sectional study; AUROC analysis, sensitivity/specificity analysis, and multivariable comparison.
performance: NEWS at threshold ≥4: Sensitivity 91.30%, Specificity 28.69%. NEWS at threshold ≥5: Sensitivity 86.96%, Specificity 45.74%. NEWS AUROC for ventilator usage: 0.750; for vasopressor usage: 0.763.
notes: Evidence extracted from text (Results/Methods), Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Table 2 (AUROC comparisons), Table 3 (Sensitivity/Specificity), and Figures 2-4 (ROC curves). NEWS outperformed other scores for all primary and secondary outcomes.
